title: Constraint conservation gated Amazon transfer

queries: confidence-gated graph representation transfer reduce negative transfer across domains
conservative transfer graph prompt alignment negative transfer gating
representation transfer confidence threshold structured prompt alignment

evidence.why_now: The first fair Part 3 screen preserved a meaningful salt-side gain but regressed both Amazon tasks. The next justified follow-up is therefore a narrower ablation of the same constraint-conservation path: keep the validated Part 1 base and the useful salt-side transfer, while explicitly softening Amazon-side transfer strength before spending Optuna budget.

hypothesis: If the first integrated constraint-conservation pass failed mainly because Amazon-side transfer was too permissive rather than because the family is intrinsically wrong, then adding confidence gating and lower transfer amplitudes on the Amazon tasks, while keeping the salt-side setting close to EXP195, should reduce over-transfer without discarding the useful conservation signal.
notes: This is a screening-only follow-up after EXP195 failed as a bundle but preserved meaningful salt-side signal.
The mechanism story stays the same; only the Amazon-side transfer is narrowed through explicit confidence gating and lower transfer amplitudes.
Do not run Optuna or final full test from this candidate directly.
The comparison remains fair at the task-hyperparameter level because the validated Phase 2 per-task best settings stay fixed and only the new Part 3 transfer strength is narrowed.

Scientific interpretation still follows the Stage 3 task-aware rule set: user-ltv subset/full-test scale mismatch still applies, and item-incoterms should still be judged scientifically against the stored full-test reference band rather than only the optimistic subset baseline.
